name: Crunchy Nut Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg white and ½ tsp water until foamy.
2. In a separate bowl, combine ¼ cup sugar and 1 tsp cinnamon.
3. Stir the sugar-cinnamon mixture and peanuts into the beaten egg white.
4. Spread the peanut mixture evenly in a buttered 13x9x2-inch baking pan.
5. Bake at 325°F for 15 minutes.
6. Stir the nuts, then bake an additional 15 minutes or until the coating is brown.
7. Cool in the pan and chop half of the nuts.
8. Store remaining sugared nuts in an airtight container or use another way.
9. For fudge, butter the sides of a 1½ quart heavy saucepan.
10. In the saucepan, combine 2 cups sugar, margarine or butter, and evaporated milk.
11. Cook over medium heat until the sugar dissolves and the mixture comes to a boil.
12. Continue cooking to 236°F (soft-ball stage), stirring only as necessary to prevent sticking (the mixture should boil gently over the entire surface).
13. Remove from heat and let cool to lukewarm (110°F) for about 1 hour.
14. Butter an 8x8x2-inch pan.
15. Add the chopped peanuts and vanilla to the fudge.
16. Beat vigorously until the fudge becomes very thick and just loses its gloss.
17. Spread the fudge in the prepared pan and score into squares while warm.
18. Cut when firm. Makes 48 pieces.
